index.html
provincial NSYT production process and assessment Chapter 1 Prerequisites

provincial NSYT production process and assessment Shuyan Jiang, Kelly Izzard, Yingbing Chen 2026-06-15 Chapter 1 Prerequisites This is the documentation of how provincial natural stand yield table (NSYT) was generated and the assessments of the product.
introduction.html
Chapter 2 Introduction

Chapter 2 Introduction Provincial NSYT is generated using Rank 1 (R1) layer of provincial VRI attributes. Variable Density Yield Projection system version 7 (VDYP7) is used for the projection. The purpose of this product is to assist in the modelling process in timber supply review and forest landscape planning. The following chapters describe how the inputs were prepared and two sets of assessments that have been done to evaluate the product: Chapter 3 describes how VDYP inputs was prepared and necessary updates/corrections of the inputs Chapter 4 shows the VDYP projection parameters and attributes that were used for the final products Chapter 5 shows the process for combining provincial managed stand yield table (MSYT) and provincial natural stand yield table (NSYT) Chapter 6 presents the comparison report which compares projected volume from provincial NSYT to the live volume in VRI R1 layer Chapter 7 presents a comparison between the polygon level volume using two types of inputs: inputs that only include R1 layer (this product, polygon level volume is the projected volume from R1 layer) and input that includes all layers (polygon level volume is the summarize of projected volume from all layers).
vdyp-inputs-preparation.html
Chapter 3 VDYP inputs preparation 3.1 load input data 3.2 Process input data

Chapter 3 VDYP inputs preparation 3.1 load input data 3.1.1 postgres database connection library(RPostgreSQL) library(data.table) library(tidyverse) library(readr) library(kableExtra) # simple connection # SCHEMA are referenced explicitly in the table names user_name <- 'postgres' data_base <- 'vdyp' con <- dbConnect('PostgreSQL',dbname=data_base,user=user_name) kablep <- function (x) { kable(x,format='markdown') } # set this up as a function run_sql <- function(fn){ system2("psql",args=c('-d',data_base,'-U',user_name,'-f',fn)) } SQLQuery <- function(query){ con <- dbConnect('PostgreSQL',dbname=data_base,user=user_name) data <- dbGetQuery(con,query) dbDisconnect(con) return(data) } 3.1.2 Load 2024 VDYP input layer table to postgres file_path <- "D:/data/projects/VDYP/VDYP/2024/vdyp_input_layer_2024.csv" file <- read_csv(file_path) colnames(file) <- tolower(colnames(file)) dbWriteTable(con, "vdyp7_input_layer_2024", file, row.names=FALSE, overwrite=TRUE) rm(file) 3.1.3 Load 2024 VDYP input polygon table to postgres file_path <- "D:/data/projects/VDYP/VDYP/2024/vdyp_input_poly_2024.csv" file <- read_csv(file_path) colnames(file) <- tolower(colnames(file)) dbWriteTable(con, "vdyp7_input_poly_2024", file, row.names=FALSE, overwrite=TRUE) rm(file) 3.1.4 Load rank1 2024 file_path <- "D:/data/projects/VDYP/VDYP/2024/rank1_2024.csv" file <- read_csv(file_path) names(file) <- tolower(names(file)) dbWriteTable(con, "rank1_2024", file, row.names=FALSE, overwrite=TRUE) rm(file) 3.1.5 Load TSA LIST file_path <- "D:/data/projects/VDYP/VDYP/2024/tsa_msyt_2025.csv" file <- read_csv(file_path) dbWriteTable(con, "fid_by_tsa_2025", file, row.names=FALSE, overwrite=TRUE) rm(file) 3.2 Process input data 3.2.1 VRI Project count for feature in list but not in polygon Are those all Private Land? check if those are non-treed from rank1 query <- "select distinct project, bclcs_level_2, count(*) from public.rank1_2024 where feature_id not in (select feature_id from vdyp7_input_poly_2024) group by 1,2" missing_poly <- dbGetQuery(con, query) kable(missing_poly,format='markdown') 3.2.2 Polygons without R1 layer assigned 4419 features missing rank1 query <- "select count(distinct feature_id) from vdyp7_input_layer_2024 where feature_id not in (select distinct feature_id from vdyp7_input_layer_2024 where forest_cover_rank_code = 1) " missing_rank <- dbGetQuery(con, query) kable(missing_rank,format='markdown') assign rank code to polygon according to VRI standard: step1: for stands only has 1 non-dead layer, assign as rank1, needs to make sure rank’s only assigned to non-dead layer step2: the first layer of a polygon (ordered by ascending layer_id) that has a crown closure greater than 10 percent and is not a D layer is assigned rank 1 step3: For polygons without layers with a crown closure above 10, the layer with the largest crown closure that is not a D layer is assigned as rank 1 step4: check the remaining features, all of them have dead/null layer_level_code only check the remaining features, all of them have dead/null layer_level_code only why are layer_level_code null - what are they? run_sql('01_fill_rank1.sql') r1 <- SQLQuery('select distinct layer_level_code, count(feature_id) from vdyp7_input_layer_2024 where feature_id not in (select distinct feature_id from vdyp7_input_layer_2024 where rank_cd_update = 1) group by 1') kablep(r1) 3.2.3 check & fix species comp for rank1 3.2.3.1 Issues Addressed Species percentage > 0 but species code is missing → removed Total species percentage ≠ 100 → normalized Duplicate species codes within a feature → aggregated Species not ordered by dominance → reordered Invalid species codes (e.g., LS → L, VW → CW) → corrected Features with no valid species → flagged 3.2.3.2 Key Processing Steps Convert wide species columns to long format for validation Remove invalid or blank species codes Standardize species and site index codes Aggregate duplicate species by summing percentages Reorder species by descending percentage Reshape back to wide format (species_cd_1–6, species_pct_1–6) Normalize percentages: If total < 100 → add difference to species 1 If total > 100 → scale all species proportionally Round percentages to 1 decimal place Track all changes using change_log 3.2.3.3 Output Table: prov_updated_spp_2024 Includes cleaned species composition and change tracking library(tidyr) library(dplyr) library(DBI) # ----------------------------------------------- # Pull source data # ----------------------------------------------- query <- " SELECT feature_id,est_site_index_species_cd, species_cd_1, species_pct_1, species_cd_2, species_pct_2, species_cd_3, species_pct_3, species_cd_4, species_pct_4, species_cd_5, species_pct_5, species_cd_6, species_pct_6 FROM vdyp7_input_layer_2024 WHERE rank_cd_update = 1; " spp_comp <- dbGetQuery(con, query) # ----------------------------------------------- # Initialize change tracking field # ----------------------------------------------- spp_comp$change_log <- NA_character_ add_change <- function(log, tag) { ifelse( is.na(log), tag, paste(log, tag, sep = " | ") ) } all_features <- spp_comp %>% distinct(feature_id) # ----------------------------------------------- # Fix outdated est_site_index_species_cd # ----------------------------------------------- spp_comp <- spp_comp %>% mutate( original_si_cd = est_site_index_species_cd, est_site_index_species_cd = case_when( toupper(est_site_index_species_cd) == "LS" ~ "L", toupper(est_site_index_species_cd) == "VW" ~ "CW", TRUE ~ toupper(est_site_index_species_cd) ) ) changed_si <- spp_comp %>% filter(original_si_cd != est_site_index_species_cd, !is.na(original_si_cd)) %>% distinct(feature_id) if (nrow(changed_si) > 0) { spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% changed_si$feature_id ] <- add_change( spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% changed_si$feature_id ], "SITE_INDEX_SPECIES_UPDATED" ) } spp_comp <- spp_comp %>% select(-original_si_cd) # ----------------------------------------------- # Pivot to long format # ----------------------------------------------- species_long_cd <- spp_comp %>% pivot_longer( cols = starts_with("species_cd_"), names_to = "rank", names_pattern = "species_cd_(\\d+)", values_to = "species_cd" ) species_long_pct <- spp_comp %>% pivot_longer( cols = starts_with("species_pct_"), names_to = "rank", names_pattern = "species_pct_(\\d+)", values_to = "percentage" ) %>% mutate(percentage = replace_na(percentage, 0)) species_long <- inner_join( species_long_cd, species_long_pct, by = c("feature_id", "rank") ) # ----------------------------------------------- # Remove invalid species codes # ----------------------------------------------- species_long <- species_long %>% filter( !is.na(species_cd), trimws(species_cd) != "" ) # ----------------------------------------------- # Detect features with NO valid species # ----------------------------------------------- valid_ids <- species_long %>% distinct(feature_id) no_valid_species_ids <- setdiff( all_features$feature_id, valid_ids$feature_id ) if (length(no_valid_species_ids) > 0) { spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% no_valid_species_ids ] <- add_change( spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% no_valid_species_ids ], "NO_VALID_SPECIES" ) } # ----------------------------------------------- # Fix outdated species codes # ----------------------------------------------- species_long <- species_long %>% mutate( original_cd = species_cd, species_cd = case_when( toupper(species_cd) == "LS" ~ "L", toupper(species_cd) == "VW" ~ "CW", TRUE ~ toupper(species_cd) ) ) changed_codes <- species_long %>% filter(original_cd != species_cd) %>% distinct(feature_id) if (nrow(changed_codes) > 0) { spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% changed_codes$feature_id ] <- add_change( spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% changed_codes$feature_id ], "SPECIES_CODE_UPDATED" ) } species_long <- species_long %>% select(-original_cd) # ----------------------------------------------- # Aggregate duplicate species # ----------------------------------------------- dup_check <- species_long %>% group_by(feature_id, species_cd) %>% tally() %>% filter(n > 1) if (nrow(dup_check) > 0) { spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% dup_check$feature_id ] <- add_change( spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% dup_check$feature_id ], "DUPLICATE_SPECIES_AGGREGATED" ) } aggregated <- species_long %>% group_by(feature_id, species_cd) %>% summarise(total_percentage = sum(percentage), .groups = "drop") # ----------------------------------------------- # Order species by descending percentage # ----------------------------------------------- aggregated <- aggregated %>% group_by(feature_id) %>% arrange(desc(total_percentage), .by_group = TRUE) %>% mutate(order = row_number()) %>% ungroup() # ----------------------------------------------- # Spread back to wide # ----------------------------------------------- df_species <- aggregated %>% select(feature_id, order, species_cd) %>% pivot_wider( names_from = order, values_from = species_cd, names_prefix = "species_cd_", values_fill = NA ) df_percentage <- aggregated %>% select(feature_id, order, total_percentage) %>% pivot_wider( names_from = order, values_from = total_percentage, names_prefix = "species_pct_", values_fill = 0 ) df_merged <- all_features %>% left_join(df_species, by = "feature_id") %>% left_join(df_percentage, by = "feature_id") # ----------------------------------------------- # Ensure pct = 0 when species_cd is NA # ----------------------------------------------- pct_cols <- grep("^species_pct_\\d+$", names(df_merged), value = TRUE) cd_cols <- gsub("pct", "cd", pct_cols) for (i in seq_along(pct_cols)) { df_merged[[pct_cols[i]]][is.na(df_merged[[cd_cols[i]]])] <- 0 } # ----------------------------------------------- # Normalization # ----------------------------------------------- df_merged$sum_pct <- rowSums(df_merged[pct_cols], na.rm = TRUE) # Under 100 idx <- df_merged$sum_pct < 100 & df_merged$sum_pct > 0 if (any(idx)) { diff_needed <- 100 - df_merged$sum_pct[idx] df_merged$species_pct_1[idx] <- df_merged$species_pct_1[idx] + diff_needed spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% df_merged$feature_id[idx] ] <- add_change( spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% df_merged$feature_id[idx] ], "UNDER100_INCREASED_SPP1" ) } # Over 100 idx <- df_merged$sum_pct > 100 if (any(idx)) { scale_factor <- 100 / df_merged$sum_pct[idx] df_merged[idx, pct_cols] <- df_merged[idx, pct_cols] * scale_factor spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% df_merged$feature_id[idx] ] <- add_change( spp_comp$change_log[ spp_comp$feature_id %in% df_merged$feature_id[idx] ], "OVER100_SCALED_DOWN" ) } # ----------------------------------------------- # Round to 1 decimal # ----------------------------------------------- df_merged[pct_cols] <- round(df_merged[pct_cols], 1) # ----------------------------------------------- # Merge change log back # ----------------------------------------------- updated_spp <- df_merged %>% left_join( spp_comp %>% select(feature_id, change_log), by = "feature_id" ) # ----------------------------------------------- # If no changes occurred # ----------------------------------------------- updated_spp$change_log[is.na(updated_spp$change_log)] <- "NO_ADJUSTMENT" # ----------------------------------------------- # Final QA Check # ----------------------------------------------- updated_spp$sum_check <- rowSums(updated_spp[pct_cols], na.rm = TRUE) issues <- updated_spp %>% filter(abs(sum_check - 100) > 0.2 & sum_check != 0) if (nrow(issues) > 0) { warning("Some rows deviate from 100% by > 0.2 — inspect.") print(issues) } # ----------------------------------------------- # Write to database # ----------------------------------------------- dbWriteTable( con, "prov_updated_spp_2024", updated_spp, row.names = FALSE, overwrite = TRUE ) 3.2.4 Update Age & Height Missing age/height for species 2: If species_pct_2 > 0 and age/height is missing → copy from species 1 Invalid ordering (species 2 > species 1): If est_age_spp2 > est_age_spp1 → reset to species 1 If est_height_spp2 > est_height_spp1 → reset to species 1 Invalid height values: If est_height_spp2 == -1 → set to 0 3.2.4.1 Output Table: prov_updated_ba_age_ht_2024 All updates are tracked in change_log library(tidyr) library(dplyr) query <- "SELECT feature_id,species_pct_2, est_age_spp1,est_height_spp1,est_age_spp2,est_height_spp2 FROM vdyp7_input_layer_2024 where rank_cd_update = 1;" layer_attri <- dbGetQuery(con, query) # ----------------------------------------------- # Initialize change tracking field # ----------------------------------------------- layer_attri$change_log <- NA_character_ add_change <- function(log, tag) { ifelse( is.na(log), tag, paste(log, tag, sep = " | ") ) } # ----------------------------------------------- # Age & Height Fixes for Species 2 # ----------------------------------------------- # 1. Copy age1 → age2 if age2 missing & species_pct_2 > 0 idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$species_pct_2) & layer_attri$species_pct_2 > 0 & is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp2) & !is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp1) layer_attri$est_age_spp2[idx] <- layer_attri$est_age_spp1[idx] layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "AGE2_missing_assign_AGE1") idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$species_pct_2) & layer_attri$species_pct_2 > 0 & is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp2) & !is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp1) layer_attri$est_height_spp2[idx] <- layer_attri$est_height_spp1[idx] layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "HT2_missing_assign_HT1") # 2. Fix age2 > age1 → copy age1 idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp2) & !is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp1) & layer_attri$est_age_spp2 > layer_attri$est_age_spp1 layer_attri$est_age_spp2[idx] <- layer_attri$est_age_spp1[idx] layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "AGE2_gt_AGE1_fixed") idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp2) & !is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp1) & layer_attri$est_height_spp2 > layer_attri$est_height_spp1 layer_attri$est_height_spp2[idx] <- layer_attri$est_height_spp1[idx] layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "HT2_gt_HT1_fixed") # 4. Replace height2 = -1 → 0 idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp2) & layer_attri$est_height_spp2 == -1 layer_attri$est_height_spp2[idx] <- 0 layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "HT2_neg1_to_0") # ----------------------------------------------- # If no changes occurred # ----------------------------------------------- layer_attri$change_log[is.na(layer_attri$change_log)] <- "NO_ADJUSTMENT" dbWriteTable(con, "prov_updated_ba_age_ht_2024", layer_attri, overwrite = TRUE, row.names = FALSE) 3.2.5 review Basal Area, Age & Height 3.2.5.1 Checks Performed Missing layer identifiers: layer_level_code is NULL vdyp7_layer_cd is NULL Basal Area issues: basal_area_75 is NULL or < 1 Age & Height issues: est_age_spp1 or est_height_spp1 is NULL est_age_spp1 > 350 (old stands, may not have yields generated) species_pct_2 > 0 but age/height missing density issues: stems_per_ha_75 is NULL or < 1 Crown closure issues: crown_closure is NULL or < 1 3.2.5.2 Output Table: prov_ba_age_ht_checks_2024 Includes all original fields plus change_log describing detected issues library(tidyr) library(dplyr) query <- "SELECT feature_id,layer_level_code, vdyp7_layer_cd, species_cd_1, species_cd_2, species_pct_2, basal_area_75, stems_per_ha_75, crown_closure, est_age_spp1,est_height_spp1,est_age_spp2,est_height_spp2 FROM vdyp7_input_layer_2024 where rank_cd_update = 1;" layer_attri <- dbGetQuery(con, query) # ----------------------------------------------- # Initialize change tracking field # ----------------------------------------------- layer_attri$change_log <- NA_character_ add_change <- function(log, tag) { ifelse( is.na(log), tag, paste(log, tag, sep = " | ") ) } # layer level code is missing idx <- is.na(layer_attri$layer_level_code) layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "missing layer level code") # vdyp7_layer_cd is missing idx <- is.na(layer_attri$vdyp7_layer_cd) layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "missing vdyp7_layer_cd") # species_cd_1 is missing idx <- is.na(layer_attri$species_cd_1) layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "missing species_cd_1") # 1. Copy age1 → age2 if age2 missing & species_pct_2 > 0 idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$species_pct_2) & layer_attri$species_pct_2 > 0 & is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp2) layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "missing est_age_spp2") idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$species_pct_2) & layer_attri$species_pct_2 > 0 & is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp2) layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "missing est_height_spp2") # 2. Fix age2 > age1 → copy age1 idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp2) & !is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp1) & layer_attri$est_age_spp2 > layer_attri$est_age_spp1 layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "est_age_spp2 > est_age_spp1") idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp2) & !is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp1) & layer_attri$est_height_spp2 > layer_attri$est_height_spp1 layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "est_height_spp2 > est_height_spp1") # 4. Replace height2 = -1 → 0 idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp2) & layer_attri$est_height_spp2 == -1 layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "est_height_spp2 < 0") # Basal Area < 1 idx <- is.na(layer_attri$basal_area_75) | layer_attri$basal_area_75 < 1 layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "BA null or lower than 1") # age1 or height 1 is null idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$species_cd_1) & is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp1) layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "missing est_height_spp1") idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$species_cd_1) & is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp1) layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "missing est_age_spp1") # # # having spp2 but age2 or height 2 is null # idx <- layer_attri$species_pct_2 > 0 & # (is.na(layer_attri$est_height_spp2) | # is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp2)) # layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "missing age2 or ht2") # old age idx <- !is.na(layer_attri$est_age_spp1) & layer_attri$est_age_spp1 > 350 layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "old age > 350") # SPH null idx <- is.na(layer_attri$stems_per_ha_75) | layer_attri$stems_per_ha_75 < 1 layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "stems_per_ha_75 null or lower than 1") # cc null idx <- is.na(layer_attri$crown_closure) | layer_attri$crown_closure < 1 layer_attri$change_log[idx] <- add_change(layer_attri$change_log[idx], "crown_closure null or lower than 1") dbWriteTable(con, "prov_ba_age_ht_checks_2024", layer_attri, overwrite = TRUE, row.names = FALSE) 3.2.6 Update Other-Veg comp Missing Data Handling: Replace all numeric NA values with 0 Flag missing BEC zone codes Vegetation Cover Adjustment: If shrub_crown_closure + herb_cover_pct + bryoid_cover_pct > 100: → calculate scaling ratio = 100 / total → apply proportional reduction using floor() → log as “VEG_SCALED_DOWN” Non-Vegetation Cover Adjustment: If sum of non_veg_cover_pct_1–3 > 100: → scale proportionally using same method → log as “NONVEG_SCALED_DOWN” Land Cover Adjustment: If sum of land_cover_pct_1–3 > 100: → scale proportionally → log as “LANDCOVER_SCALED_DOWN” Ratio Tracking: Store applied scaling ratios for: vegetation (ratio_veg) non-vegetation (ratio_non_veg) land cover (ratio_land) Change Logging: All adjustments tracked in change_log Records with no changes labeled as “NO_ADJUSTMENT” 3.2.6.1 Output Table: prov_updated_other_veg_2024 Includes adjusted values, totals, ratios, and change tracking library(dplyr) library(DBI) query <- " SELECT feature_id, bec_zone_code, shrub_crown_closure, herb_cover_pct, bryoid_cover_pct, non_veg_cover_pct_1, non_veg_cover_pct_2, non_veg_cover_pct_3, land_cover_pct_1, land_cover_pct_2, land_cover_pct_3 FROM vdyp7_input_poly_2024; " df <- dbGetQuery(con, query) # ----------------------------------------------- # Initialize change tracking field # ----------------------------------------------- df$change_log <- NA_character_ add_change <- function(log, tag) { ifelse( is.na(log), tag, paste(log, tag, sep = " | ") ) } # ----------------------------------------------- # Check missing BEC zone # ----------------------------------------------- bec_missing_idx <- is.na(df$bec_zone_code) | trimws(df$bec_zone_code) == "" if (any(bec_missing_idx)) { df$change_log[bec_missing_idx] <- add_change(df$change_log[bec_missing_idx], "MISSING_BEC") } # ----------------------------------------------- # Replace numeric NA with 0 # ----------------------------------------------- numeric_cols <- setdiff(names(df), c("feature_id", "bec_zone_code", "change_log")) df[, numeric_cols][is.na(df[, numeric_cols])] <- 0 # ----------------------------------------------- # Vegetation Layer Scaling # ----------------------------------------------- df$total_veg <- df$shrub_crown_closure + df$herb_cover_pct + df$bryoid_cover_pct veg_idx <- df$total_veg > 100 if (any(veg_idx)) { ratio <- 100 / df$total_veg[veg_idx] df$shrub_crown_closure[veg_idx] <- floor(df$shrub_crown_closure[veg_idx] * ratio) df$herb_cover_pct[veg_idx] <- floor(df$herb_cover_pct[veg_idx] * ratio) df$bryoid_cover_pct[veg_idx] <- floor(df$bryoid_cover_pct[veg_idx] * ratio) df$change_log[veg_idx] <- add_change(df$change_log[veg_idx], "VEG_SCALED_DOWN") } df$ratio_veg <- ifelse(df$total_veg > 100, 100 / df$total_veg, 1) # ----------------------------------------------- # Non-Vegetated Cover Scaling # ----------------------------------------------- df$total_non_veg <- df$non_veg_cover_pct_1 + df$non_veg_cover_pct_2 + df$non_veg_cover_pct_3 nonveg_idx <- df$total_non_veg > 100 if (any(nonveg_idx)) { ratio <- 100 / df$total_non_veg[nonveg_idx] df$non_veg_cover_pct_1[nonveg_idx] <- floor(df$non_veg_cover_pct_1[nonveg_idx] * ratio) df$non_veg_cover_pct_2[nonveg_idx] <- floor(df$non_veg_cover_pct_2[nonveg_idx] * ratio) df$non_veg_cover_pct_3[nonveg_idx] <- floor(df$non_veg_cover_pct_3[nonveg_idx] * ratio) df$change_log[nonveg_idx] <- add_change(df$change_log[nonveg_idx], "NONVEG_SCALED_DOWN") } df$ratio_non_veg <- ifelse(df$total_non_veg > 100, 100 / df$total_non_veg, 1) # ----------------------------------------------- # Land Cover Scaling # ----------------------------------------------- df$total_land <- df$land_cover_pct_1 + df$land_cover_pct_2 + df$land_cover_pct_3 land_idx <- df$total_land > 100 if (any(land_idx)) { ratio <- 100 / df$total_land[land_idx] df$land_cover_pct_1[land_idx] <- floor(df$land_cover_pct_1[land_idx] * ratio) df$land_cover_pct_2[land_idx] <- floor(df$land_cover_pct_2[land_idx] * ratio) df$land_cover_pct_3[land_idx] <- floor(df$land_cover_pct_3[land_idx] * ratio) df$change_log[land_idx] <- add_change(df$change_log[land_idx], "LANDCOVER_SCALED_DOWN") } df$ratio_land <- ifelse(df$total_land > 100, 100 / df$total_land, 1) # ----------------------------------------------- # If no changes occurred # ----------------------------------------------- df$change_log[is.na(df$change_log)] <- "NO_ADJUSTMENT" # ----------------------------------------------- # Final Output # ----------------------------------------------- updated_df <- df %>% select( feature_id, bec_zone_code, shrub_crown_closure, herb_cover_pct, bryoid_cover_pct, non_veg_cover_pct_1, non_veg_cover_pct_2, non_veg_cover_pct_3, land_cover_pct_1, land_cover_pct_2, land_cover_pct_3, total_veg, ratio_veg, total_non_veg, ratio_non_veg, total_land, ratio_land, change_log ) dbWriteTable( con, "prov_updated_other_veg_2024", updated_df, overwrite = TRUE, row.names = FALSE )
vdyp-projection-parameters-and-final-yield-table-development.html
Chapter 4 VDYP projection parameters and final yield table development

Chapter 4 VDYP projection parameters and final yield table development VDYP7 console version: 7.17d projection parameters: parameters value time series age 0-250 years old time increments 10 years reported utilization level 12.5cm pine and deciduous, 17.5cm for all other species calculated attributes in the final NSYT conifer only live volume: conifer only live volume is calculated by adding up the projected volume of the conifer portion of each FEATURE_ID. deciduous only live volume: deciduous only live volume is calculated by adding up the projected volume of the deciduous portion of each FEATURE_ID. conifer height and deciduous height: VDYP7 doesn’t project conifer height and deciduous height. A height ratio between conifer height and deciduous height is developed using managed stand yield table by BEC_ZONE and BEC variants, and the ratio was applied to the PRJ_DOM_HT to estimate conifer height and deciduous height.
provincial-yield-table---nsyt-and-msyt-combined-table.html

Chapter 5 Provincial Yield table - NSYT and MSYT combined table 5.1 Overview 5.2 Define Deciduous Species 5.3 Load VDYP Yield Table 5.4 Extract Species Codes 5.5 Extract Projected Volumes 5.6 Calculate Conifer and Deciduous Volume 5.7 Convert Volume Table to Wide Format 5.8 Load MSYT Tables 5.9 Extend MSYT Curves to Age 250 5.10 Reorder MSYT Columns 5.11 Build MSYT Output 5.12 Load Height Ratios 5.13 Calculate VDYP Heights 5.14 Build VDYP Output 5.15 Export Final Yield Table 5.16 QA/QC Checks 5.17 Review NSYT Zero-Volume Records 5.18 Review MSYT Zero-Volume Records

Chapter 5 Provincial Yield table - NSYT and MSYT combined table 5.1 Overview This script generates a combined yield table containing: Conifer merchantable volume (MVcon_*) Deciduous merchantable volume (MVdec_*) Conifer dominant height (HTcon_*) Deciduous dominant height (HTdec_*) The workflow combines: VDYP-based yield curves (NSYT workflow) MSYT yield curves The final output is exported as a single yield table for downstream modeling. ## Load Libraries library(data.table) library(dplyr) library(tidyr) 5.2 Define Deciduous Species Species in this list are treated as deciduous. decid <- c( "AC","ACB","ACT","AT","AD","AX", "DR","E","EA","EB","EE","EP","ES","EW", "EXP","XH","G","GP","KC","L","LA","LT", "LW","M","MB","MN","MV","Q","QG","XH", "WT","WS","WP","WD","WB","WA","W", "VP","VB","V","U","RA" ) NoDecidVol_calculator() calculates conifer-only projected volume by removing deciduous species from VDYP projected species volumes. NoDecidVol_calculator <- function (sp_list, vol_list, decidsp, multilayer = FALSE) { # Standardize species slot names sp_list[spcode %in% "SPECIES_1_CODE", `:=`(spcode, "sp1")] sp_list[spcode %in% "SPECIES_2_CODE", `:=`(spcode, "sp2")] sp_list[spcode %in% "SPECIES_3_CODE", `:=`(spcode, "sp3")] sp_list[spcode %in% "SPECIES_4_CODE", `:=`(spcode, "sp4")] sp_list[spcode %in% "SPECIES_5_CODE", `:=`(spcode, "sp5")] sp_list[spcode %in% "SPECIES_6_CODE", `:=`(spcode, "sp6")] # Remove deciduous species sp_list2 <- sp_list[ !is.na(sp) & sp != "" & !sp %in% decidsp ] # Replace NA projected volumes with 0 vol_list[is.na(PRJ_SP1_VOL_DWB), `:=`(PRJ_SP1_VOL_DWB, 0)] vol_list[is.na(PRJ_SP2_VOL_DWB), `:=`(PRJ_SP2_VOL_DWB, 0)] vol_list[is.na(PRJ_SP3_VOL_DWB), `:=`(PRJ_SP3_VOL_DWB, 0)] vol_list[is.na(PRJ_SP4_VOL_DWB), `:=`(PRJ_SP4_VOL_DWB, 0)] vol_list[is.na(PRJ_SP5_VOL_DWB), `:=`(PRJ_SP5_VOL_DWB, 0)] vol_list[is.na(PRJ_SP6_VOL_DWB), `:=`(PRJ_SP6_VOL_DWB, 0)] # Convert projected species volumes to long format vol_list2 <- melt( vol_list, id.vars = c("FEATURE_ID", "PRJ_TOTAL_AGE"), measure.vars = c( "PRJ_SP1_VOL_DWB", "PRJ_SP2_VOL_DWB", "PRJ_SP3_VOL_DWB", "PRJ_SP4_VOL_DWB", "PRJ_SP5_VOL_DWB", "PRJ_SP6_VOL_DWB" ), variable.name = "spcode", value.name = "vol" ) # Standardize projected volume species names vol_list2[spcode %in% "PRJ_SP1_VOL_DWB", `:=`(spcode, "sp1")] vol_list2[spcode %in% "PRJ_SP2_VOL_DWB", `:=`(spcode, "sp2")] vol_list2[spcode %in% "PRJ_SP3_VOL_DWB", `:=`(spcode, "sp3")] vol_list2[spcode %in% "PRJ_SP4_VOL_DWB", `:=`(spcode, "sp4")] vol_list2[spcode %in% "PRJ_SP5_VOL_DWB", `:=`(spcode, "sp5")] vol_list2[spcode %in% "PRJ_SP6_VOL_DWB", `:=`(spcode, "sp6")] # Merge species and volume tables all <- merge( sp_list2, vol_list2, by = c("FEATURE_ID", "spcode"), all.x = TRUE ) all2 <- all[ , .(PRJ_VOL_DWB_conifer = sum(vol, na.rm = TRUE)), by = c("FEATURE_ID", "PRJ_TOTAL_AGE") ] return(all2) } 5.3 Load VDYP Yield Table yld <- readRDS( "D:\\data\\projects\\VDYP\\VDYP\\2024\\prov\\VDYP7RunParallel\\prov_lesschanges_nsyt_yieldtable.rds" ) setDT(yld) 5.4 Extract Species Codes Convert species fields into long format. d <- unique( yld[, .( FEATURE_ID, SPECIES_1_CODE, SPECIES_2_CODE, SPECIES_3_CODE, SPECIES_4_CODE, SPECIES_5_CODE, SPECIES_6_CODE )] ) d2 <- melt( d, id.vars = "FEATURE_ID", measure.vars = c( "SPECIES_1_CODE", "SPECIES_2_CODE", "SPECIES_3_CODE", "SPECIES_4_CODE", "SPECIES_5_CODE", "SPECIES_6_CODE" ), variable.name = "spcode", value.name = "sp" ) 5.5 Extract Projected Volumes e <- unique( yld[, .( FEATURE_ID, PRJ_TOTAL_AGE, PRJ_SP1_VOL_DWB, PRJ_SP2_VOL_DWB, PRJ_SP3_VOL_DWB, PRJ_SP4_VOL_DWB, PRJ_SP5_VOL_DWB, PRJ_SP6_VOL_DWB )] ) t <- unique( yld[, .( FEATURE_ID, PRJ_TOTAL_AGE, PRJ_VOL_DWB )] ) 5.6 Calculate Conifer and Deciduous Volume f <- NoDecidVol_calculator( sp_list = d2, vol_list = e, decidsp = decid ) final <- t %>% left_join(f, by = join_by(FEATURE_ID, PRJ_TOTAL_AGE)) %>% mutate( PRJ_VOL_DWB = round(PRJ_VOL_DWB, 2), PRJ_VOL_DWB_conifer = round(PRJ_VOL_DWB_conifer, 2), PRJ_VOL_DWB_decid = PRJ_VOL_DWB - coalesce(PRJ_VOL_DWB_conifer, 0) ) 5.7 Convert Volume Table to Wide Format final_wide <- final %>% mutate( age_label = paste0("_", PRJ_TOTAL_AGE) ) %>% pivot_wider( id_cols = FEATURE_ID, names_from = age_label, values_from = c( PRJ_VOL_DWB_conifer, PRJ_VOL_DWB_decid ), names_glue = "{ifelse(.value == 'PRJ_VOL_DWB_conifer', 'MVcon', 'MVdec')}{age_label}" ) 5.8 Load MSYT Tables msyt <- read.csv( "D://data//projects//MSYTDelivery//msyt_2025//prov//MSYT_prov_current_input_output.csv" ) reference <- read.csv( "D://data//projects//MSYTDelivery//msyt_2025//prov//MSYT_prov_reference.csv" ) msyt_fut_in <- read.csv( "D://data//projects//MSYTDelivery//msyt_2025//prov//MSYT_prov_future_input.csv" ) 5.9 Extend MSYT Curves to Age 250 Ages greater than 120 are populated using age-120 values. new_ages <- seq(130, 250, 10) msyt_expanded <- msyt for (age in new_ages) { msyt_expanded <- msyt_expanded %>% mutate( !!paste0("MVcon_", age) := MVcon_120, !!paste0("MVdec_", age) := MVdec_120, !!paste0("HTcon_", age) := HTcon_120, !!paste0("HTdec_", age) := HTdec_120 ) } 5.10 Reorder MSYT Columns ages <- seq(0, 250, 10) ordered_cols <- c( "feature_id", paste0("MVcon_", ages), paste0("MVdec_", ages), paste0("HTcon_", ages), paste0("HTdec_", ages) ) msyt_expanded <- msyt_expanded %>% select(all_of(ordered_cols)) 5.11 Build MSYT Output msyt_part <- reference %>% filter(!current_yield %in% c("VDYP", "Excluded")) %>% select("feature_id") %>% left_join(msyt_expanded, by = "feature_id") %>% mutate(source = "MSYT") 5.12 Load Height Ratios ht_ratio <- read.csv( "D://data//projects//VDYP//VDYP//2024//prov//bec_height_ratios.csv" ) 5.13 Calculate VDYP Heights Calculate Conifer and Deciduous Heights based on the ratio table vdyp_ht <- unique( yld[, .( FEATURE_ID, PRJ_TOTAL_AGE, PRJ_DOM_HT, SPECIES_1_CODE )] ) vdyp_ht <- vdyp_ht %>% left_join( reference %>% select(feature_id, bec_zone, bec_subzone) %>% mutate( bec_label = paste0(bec_zone, "_", bec_subzone) ), by = c("FEATURE_ID" = "feature_id") ) vdyp_ht <- vdyp_ht %>% left_join(ht_ratio, by = "bec_label") vdyp_ht <- vdyp_ht %>% mutate( is_decid = SPECIES_1_CODE %in% decid, HTcon = case_when( is_decid ~ PRJ_DOM_HT * con_dec_ratio, TRUE ~ PRJ_DOM_HT ), HTdec = case_when( is_decid ~ PRJ_DOM_HT, TRUE ~ PRJ_DOM_HT * dec_con_ratio ) ) vdyp_ht_wide <- vdyp_ht %>% mutate( age_label = paste0("_", PRJ_TOTAL_AGE) ) %>% pivot_wider( id_cols = FEATURE_ID, names_from = age_label, values_from = c(HTcon, HTdec), names_glue = "{.value}{age_label}" ) 5.14 Build VDYP Output vdyp_part <- reference %>% filter(current_yield %in% c("VDYP", "Excluded")) %>% select("feature_id") %>% left_join( final_wide, by = c("feature_id" = "FEATURE_ID") ) %>% left_join( vdyp_ht_wide, by = c("feature_id" = "FEATURE_ID") ) %>% mutate(source = "NSYT") final_yld <- bind_rows( msyt_part, vdyp_part ) 5.15 Export Final Yield Table write.csv( final_yld, "D:\\data\\projects\\VDYP\\VDYP\\2024\\prov\\yld_combined.csv", row.names = FALSE ) 5.16 QA/QC Checks Identify records where all merchantable volume columns equal 0. mv_cols <- grep( "^MV(con|dec)_", names(final_yld), value = TRUE ) zero_features <- final_yld %>% mutate( total_mv = rowSums( across(all_of(mv_cols)), na.rm = TRUE ) ) %>% filter(total_mv == 0) %>% select(feature_id, source) 5.17 Review NSYT Zero-Volume Records nsyt_all_zero <- zero_features %>% filter(source == "NSYT") %>% left_join(reference, by = "feature_id") %>% left_join(t, by = c("feature_id" = "FEATURE_ID")) nsyt_all_zero 5.18 Review MSYT Zero-Volume Records msyt_all_zero <- zero_features %>% filter(source == "MSYT") %>% left_join(reference, by = "feature_id") %>% left_join(msyt, by = "feature_id") msyt_all_zero
comparison-with-vri-r1.html
Chapter 6 Comparison with VRI R1 6.1 Get data 6.2 Closest Match

Chapter 6 Comparison with VRI R1 The following contrasts the Beta version of the 2026 Provincial Natural Stand Yield Table (NSYT) dataset with the currently published production year 2025 (projection year Dec 2024) veg_comp_lyr_r1_poly_2024 (R1). The R1 contains 6872386 features, 90% of which (6209645) has a species identified and (spec_cd_1) and 89% (6140967) remain after initial cleaning. library(DBI) library(RPostgreSQL) library(tidyverse) library(terra) library(R.utils) library(tidytext) library(arsenal) library(dlookr) library(gdalUtilities) library(glue) library(janitor) library(knitr) library(kableExtra) library(tidytext) library(fs) library(data.table) library(glue) library(janitor) library(dplyr) rm(list = ls()) options(scipen = 999) set.seed(123) # source("log_files.R") knitr::opts_chunk$set( echo = TRUE, # Show code in the output fig.width = 4, # Set the width of the figures (in inches) fig.height = 4, # Set the height of the figures (in inches) collapse = TRUE, # Collapsing code and output # out.width = '250px', # Standardize output image width # out.height = '250px', # Standardize output image height message = FALSE, # Hide messages warning = FALSE # Hide warnings ) 6.1 Get data The following snippet loads an existing VDYP from an RDS file containing 6140967 features, and joins it with the data pulled from the MSYT postgres database based on feature_id. Various new columns are created, including estimated volumes (vri_vol), capped age (vri_age250), and interpolated volumes (vdyp_vol) based on age ranges. The dataset is cleaned and transformed using dplyr functions. Finally, the script calculates the closest volume match for each feature by comparing two volume estimates (vdyp_vol and prj_vol_dwb), selecting the record with the smallest difference between the two. The result is a refined dataset (nsyt_closest) where each feature has a matched volume estimate, and a new column (vol_dif) is added to represent the difference between the two volume estimates. This processing likely aids in vegetation analysis, volume estimation, or model calibration based on multiple data sources. schema <- 'msyt_2025' opt <- paste0("-c search_path=",schema) user_name <- 'results' database <- 'msyt' db<- dbConnect('PostgreSQL',dbname=database,user=user_name,options = opt) prov<-readRDS("./data/rds/prov_nsyt2026.rds") sql<-glue_sql("select feature_id, proj_age_1 as age, species_cd_1 as leading_species, species_cd_2 as second_species, live_stand_volume_125 as r1_vol125, live_stand_volume_175 as r1_vol175, dead_stand_volume_125 as r1_dvol125 from veg_comp_fdw_full",.con = db) r1<-dbGetQuery(db,sql) ############################################################################## # # prov_df<-inner_join(prov_df,r1,join_by("feature_id")) prov_df2 <- prov_df %>% mutate( vri_vol = case_when( grepl("^P", leading_species) ~ r1_vol125, grepl("^A", leading_species) ~ r1_vol125, grepl("^E", leading_species) ~ r1_vol125, grepl("^D", leading_species) ~ r1_vol125, grepl("^M", leading_species) ~ r1_vol125, grepl("^W", leading_species) ~ r1_vol125, TRUE ~ r1_vol175 # Otherwise, assign r1_vol175 ), across(where(is.numeric), ~replace_na(., 0)), vri_age250 = pmin(age, 250), age_low = floor(vri_age250/10) * 10, age_high = ceiling(vri_age250/10) * 10, p_low = (age_high - vri_age250) / 10 ) %>% group_by(feature_id) %>% mutate( l_vol = prj_vol_dwb[match(age_low, prj_total_age)], u_vol = prj_vol_dwb[match(age_high, prj_total_age)], vdyp_vol = (p_low * l_vol) + ((1 - p_low) * u_vol) ) %>% select(feature_id, vri_vol,l_vol, u_vol, vdyp_vol)%>% slice(1)%>% ungroup() nsyt<-inner_join(prov_df,prov_df2,join_by("feature_id")) nsyt_closest <- nsyt %>% filter(!is.na(vdyp_vol), !is.na(prj_vol_dwb)) %>% group_by(feature_id) %>% slice_min(abs(prj_vol_dwb - vdyp_vol), n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>% ungroup()%>% mutate(vol_dif = vri_vol - vdyp_vol 6.2 Closest Match The closest match volume calculation works by identifying the record with the smallest absolute difference between two volume estimates for each feature. Specifically, the script compares two columns: vdyp_vol (a volume estimate derived from a linear interpolation of two decadal volumes based on age [proj_age_1]) and prj_vol_dwb (the decadal VDYP volume). Filtering for Valid Volumes: The script first filters out any rows where either vdyp_vol or prj_vol_dwb is NA, ensuring that only valid rows with both volume estimates are considered for comparison. Grouping by feature_id: The data is grouped by feature_id, meaning the script performs the closest match calculation separately for each feature (or record). Identifying the Closest Match: For each group (feature_id), the script calculates the absolute difference between vdyp_vol and prj_vol_dwb using the expression abs(prj_vol_dwb - vdyp_vol). The row with the smallest difference is considered the “closest match” for that feature. This is achieved using the slice_min function, which selects the row where the absolute difference is the smallest (n = 1), ensuring that ties (rows with the same smallest difference) are excluded (with_ties = FALSE). Calculating the Volume Difference: Once the closest match is found, a new column vol_dif is created, which represents the difference between the VDYP derived lineral interpolation and the R1 volume: vol_dif = vri_vol - vdyp_vol.The output is then saved as a RDS file. The resulting NSYT closest data frame is loaded to disk and its vol_dif field is then assessed statistically using the dlookr::describe function. prov<-readRDS("../nsyt_closest.rds") # pretty_table(describe(prov)) kable(describe(prov), caption = "Volume Comparison Table", format = "html", booktabs = TRUE) %>% kable_styling(full_width = TRUE, font_size = 10) %>% scroll_box(width = "100%") (#tab:tab-closest_match)Volume Comparison Table described_variables n na mean sd se_mean IQR skewness kurtosis p00 p01 p05 p10 p20 p25 p30 p40 p50 p60 p70 p75 p80 p90 p95 p99 p100 feature_id 0.26338 1.145801 3.128867 11.7303 1771.781 Of the 6140967 features assessed the mean difference in volume between the VDYP dataset and the R1 is -1.8m3 (sd: 11.13), the median difference is -0.015m3 , 986984 (16%) of the features have volume difference > +/- 5m3, 454585 (8%) features have volume difference > +/- 10m3 and 170037 (2.7%) features have volume difference > +/- 20m3. The are a number of sources that drive divergence including approaches to interpolation (most common source for volume differences < 5m3), age cutoffs and layer/project table input error in both the R1 and vdyp_vol dataset. The provincial VDYP dataset has an age cutoff of 250 years effecting 244710 (25%) of the 986984 of the features have volume difference > +/- 5m3. 85% of the features > 250 years of age have vdyp volumes > the R1 volume suggesting the much older stands in the R1 are decrementing volume, driving the divergence. There are 15270 features that have ages > 250 and vdyp_vols == 0. Of the features < 250 years of age 742274 (12%) of the features have volume difference > +/- 5m3, 285508 (4.6%) features have volume difference > +/- 10m3 and 70880 (1%) features have volume difference > +/- 20m3. There 9935 features where the vri_vol == 0 and the vdyp_vol > 0 and 365 features where the vdyp_vol == 0. 112262 features < 250 years have R1 yields more reflective of the layer table est_age_1 and 2 fields than the projected age (proj_age_1) suggesting an error has occured in the 2025 update.
comparison-with-combined-mulitple-layers-projection.html

Chapter 7 comparison with combined mulitple layers’ projection 7.1 set1. r1 only vs multilayer set1 (containing P layer and at least one other layer that is not D layer) 7.2 set2. r1 only vs multilayer set2 (only containing P layer and D layer) 7.3 Overall - one-layer polygons + multiple-layer polygons (set1+set2)

Chapter 7 comparison with combined mulitple layers’ projection Some of the VRI polygons contain multiple layers, for example, a polygon might have a primary layer (also labeled as R1 layer) and a residual layer. VDYP7 can projected these polygons by each of the layer and return yield projection for each layer within each polygon. The final yield of each polygon is the summarized projection of each layer to the polygon level. For the process of provincial NSYT, only R1 layer was input into VDYP and being projected. The projected yield from “R1-only” input would be different from the aggregated yield from each layer. This chapter is to compare the summarized yields of each layer with the R1 layer only projection. Table 1. numbers multi-layer polygons across the province (from vdyp_input_layer_and_polygon_2024) category N pct onelayer 5115447 83 multilayer 239892 4 P_and_D 812801 13 *“multilayer” means the polygon has P layer and at least one other layer that is not D layer; “P_and_D” means the polygon only has P layer and D layer 7.1 set1. r1 only vs multilayer set1 (containing P layer and at least one other layer that is not D layer) Number of multi-layer polygons that were included in this comparison: 239198 Mean projected volume net dead, waste and breakage (PRJ_VOL_DWB) across projected stand age were calculated and compared between multiple-layer projection and R1-only projection. The mean PRJ_VOL_DWB was very similar between the two projections, with the multiple-layer projection generally producing slightly higher mean values at younger ages and converging with the R1-only projection at older ages (Figure 1 and Figure 2). Even though mean PRJ_VOL_DWB was close, the individual differences can vary widely in both positive and negative directions and the variation increases over projected age (Figure 3). Figure 1. Mean PRJ_VOL_DWB by projected age Figure 2. Mean differences of PRJ_VOL_DWB by projected age Figure 3. Variation of PRJ_VOL_DWB by projected age 7.2 set2. r1 only vs multilayer set2 (only containing P layer and D layer) Number of multi-layer polygons that were included in this comparison: 811118 The projection of P layer from multiple-layer set 2, of which each polygon contains one P layer and one D layer, was compared with R1-only projection. “R1-only” projection produces consistently higher mean PRJ_VOL_DWB than multiple-layer projection after approximately age 25, and the mean differences increased with the increase of projected age (Figure 4 and Figure 5). Individual differences varied widely mainly in the positive direction and the variation increases over projected age (Figure 6). Figure 4. Mean PRJ_VOL_DWB by projected age Figure 5. Mean differences of PRJ_VOL_DWB by projected age Figure 6. Variation of PRJ_VOL_DWB by projected age 7.3 Overall - one-layer polygons + multiple-layer polygons (set1+set2) Mean PRJ_VOL_DWB were calculated by averaging the PRJ_VOL_DWB from all polygons provincially. At the provincial scale, “R1-only” projection produced higher mean PRJ_VOL_DWB (Figure 7), which is mainly driven by the 13% “P_and_D” polygons (from Table 1). Differences increase with projected age and the largest differences from this test is around 3m3/ha at age 200 (Table 2). Figure 7.
